edges.mechanism: ABA signaling activates ABI5 bZIP transcription factor
edges.evidence: transcript levels of all four genes were downregulated in aba2 with ABI5 most suppressed
ABI5 directly binds to two discrete regions in the SHB1 promoter
SHB1 RNA accumulation peaks disappeared upon treatment with 100 uM ABA
delayed endosperm cellularization in aba2 caused increased nuclear proliferation and larger seeds
ABA2 is upstream biosynthesis enzyme; loss leads to reduced ABA and larger seeds
NCED is the rate-limiting biosynthetic enzyme for ABA
CYP707A catalyses ABA catabolism

edges.mechanism: ABA importer - reduces ABA accumulation in embryo by importing to neighboring tissues
edges.evidence: double-knockdown resulted in lower ABA accumulation in the embryo; increased overall seed size
ABCG17/ABCG18 redundantly regulate seed size
ABA-deficient mutants produced seeds with increased size, mass, and embryo cell number but delayed endosperm cellularization

edges.mechanism: positive regulation of ABI3 protein abundance
edges.evidence: FUS3 and LEC1 regulate positively the abundance of the ABI3 protein in the seed
FUS3 and LEC1 regulate positively the abundance of the ABI3 protein in the seed
ABI3 and FUS3 positively regulate themselves and each other, thereby forming feedback loops essential for their sustained and uniform expression in the embryo
One of the major roles of LEC2 was to upregulate FUS3 and ABI3
One of the major roles of LEC2 was to upregulate FUS3 and ABI3
Expression of individual members of the 12S storage protein gene family is controlled by ABI3
ChIP-chip assays confirmed that AtWRI1 is a direct target of LEC1
Induction of LEC2 activates AtWRI1 expression via inducible transgenic plants
ChIP-chip assays provided evidence validating AtWRI1 as a direct target gene of FUS3
ChIP experiments validated that MYB89 binds to the AtWRI1 promoter

edges.mechanism: phosphorylation at T70/S166 promotes proteasomal degradation
edges.evidence: AtWRI1 phosphorylation triggered by KIN10 is critical for degradation of AtWRI1

edges.mechanism: binds WRI1 and blocks BPM-mediated degradation
edges.evidence: Overexpression of 14-3-3 increased AtWRI1-regulated oil biosynthesis and enhanced protein stability

edges.mechanism: master regulator of fatty acid biosynthesis
edges.evidence: wri1-1 mutant displays 80% reduced seed oil content
WRI1 master regulator of storage oil; loss reduces filling and final seed mass

edges.mechanism: DELLA stimulates ABI5 expression in seed
edges.evidence: The GA Signaling Repressor RGL2 Inhibits Arabidopsis Seed Germination by Stimulating Abscisic Acid Synthesis and ABI5 Activity

edges.mechanism: DELLA RGL2 stimulates ABA synthesis (via NCED)
edges.evidence: RGL2 inhibits germination by stimulating ABA synthesis and ABI5 activity

edges.mechanism: ABA signaling output restricts seed size via SHB1 repression
edges.evidence: abi5 mutant exhibits larger seeds; enhanced SHB1 expression

edges.mechanism: GA20ox/GA3ox catalyze last steps of GA biosynthesis
edges.evidence: GA20-oxidases and GA 3-oxidases catalyze the last steps of GA biosynthesis converting inactive GA precursors into bioactive GAs
GA2ox enzymes catabolize active GAs

perturbations.evidence_sentence: abi5 mutant exhibits larger seeds; seed size increase 10-15%
aba2 mutant seed size increase 25-30%; delayed endosperm cellularization; upregulated SHB1, MINI3, IKU2
mir17,18 significantly larger seeds; increased seed width and length; wider siliques
abi3 mutants show seed maturation defects and reduced storage protein accumulation
fus3 mutants show heterochronic development; altered pigmentation and storage protein accumulation
lec1 mutants lose cotyledon identity and late embryogenesis defects
lec2 shows dramatic decrease in ABI3 and FUS3 expression; maturation defects
wri1-1 mutant displays 80% reduced seed oil content; reduced seed filling
WRI1 overexpression enhances oil biosynthesis and seed weight
GA-deficient mutants have stabilized DELLA, increased seed size via DELLA positive regulation
